name: Adipocyte GHR disruption improved advanced-age metabolism and male frailty/lifespan measures
summary: Adipocyte-specific GHR disruption supports tissue-specific GH/IGF modulation rather than uniform systemic suppression.
statement: Adipocyte-specific growth hormone receptor disruption improved advanced-age insulin sensitivity and produced male-biased improvements in grip strength, frailty measures, and lifespan-related outcomes.
population_or_model: Mice with adipocyte-specific growth hormone receptor disruption
time_horizon: Aging mouse follow-up into advanced age
quantitative_note: The source emphasizes sex- and tissue-specific effects.
caveats: This is tissue-specific mouse genetic evidence.
The male-biased functional and lifespan signals do not justify broad systemic GH/IGF suppression.
Translation would require tissue-selective strategies and safety testing.
